faults.name: Capacitor value is wrong
faults.why: A capacitor that is too large moves the cutoff lower and causes unexpected attenuation at frequencies that should pass.
faults.verification_test: Measure or read the capacitor marking, then compare gain at a known frequency below cutoff.
faults.fix_recipe: Install the intended capacitor value and recompute fc = 1/(2*pi*R*C).

faults.name: Input and output nodes are swapped
faults.why: Probing the resistor side or driving the capacitor node can make the filter response look inverted or attenuated.
faults.verification_test: Confirm Vin is applied before R and Vout is measured at the R-C junction.
faults.fix_recipe: Move the source and probe to the documented nodes.

faults.name: Capacitor ground leg is open
faults.why: An open capacitor return removes the intended shunt path and makes the measured response inconsistent.
faults.verification_test: Power off and check continuity from capacitor ground leg to circuit ground.
faults.fix_recipe: Reconnect the capacitor return to the shared ground node.
